title: OSC 7 — Report current working directory (file:// URL)
summary: OSC 7 ; file://host/path ST tells the terminal the shell's current working directory as a file URL. Terminals use it to open new tabs/splits in the same directory and to resolve relative paths in hyperlinks. Emitted by the shell from a precmd/PROMPT_COMMAND hook. Originated with iTerm2; now broadly supported.

attribution.note: iTerm2 documents OSC 7 ; file://hostname/path as the way the shell reports its current directory so iTerm2 can open new sessions in the same place.
VTE (GNOME Terminal) documents OSC 7 file:// URI as the current-directory / current-file URI used to open new terminals in the same directory.
WezTerm shell-integration docs describe emitting OSC 7 ; file://HOST/CWD so WezTerm knows the cwd for spawning tabs/panes (second independent source for the file:// form).

frame.note: OSC frame per ECMA-48 5th ed. 1991 / ISO 6429. Command number 7 is an iTerm2-originated de-facto convention with no formal registry.

terminator_detail.note: ECMA-48 mandates ST; terminals also accept BEL. Emit ST, accept both.

params.name: Report cwd
params.meaning: OSC 7 ; file://HOST/PATH ST reports the shell's current working directory. HOST is the hostname (so the terminal can tell local vs remote/ssh) and PATH is the absolute, percent-encoded path. The shell emits this from precmd / PROMPT_COMMAND after each directory change.
params.byte_sequence_ST_readable: ESC ] 7 ; f i l e : / / m y h o s t / h o m e / u s e r / p r o j e c t ESC \   ==  \x1b]7;file://myhost/home/user/project\x1b\\
params.byte_sequence_BEL_readable: ESC ] 7 ; f i l e : / / m y h o s t / . . . BEL   ==  \x1b]7;file://myhost/home/user/project\x07

support_matrix.notes: Origin of OSC 7 cwd reporting; opens new sessions in the reported directory.
Uses the file:// URI to spawn new terminals in the same directory.
Reads OSC 7 to spawn tabs/panes in the current directory.
Honors OSC 7 for new-window cwd inheritance.
Uses OSC 7 to duplicate tabs in the same working directory.

gotchas: The PATH component must be percent-encoded per RFC 3986: spaces become %20, etc. Emitting a raw space or other reserved char yields a malformed URL the terminal may ignore.
Include the hostname so the terminal can distinguish a local cwd from one over ssh; an empty host (file:///path) is interpreted as localhost.
OSC 7 reports the directory; it does NOT mark prompt/command boundaries — that is OSC 133. They are commonly emitted together from the same precmd hook.
OSC 7 is distinct from OSC 8 (hyperlinks) and from OSC 6 (some terminals' 'current file' / document URI); do not conflate.
Emit it from precmd / the PROMPT_COMMAND, after cd, so the terminal's notion of cwd stays in sync with the shell.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: OSC 7 form carries a file:// URL and is a syntactically valid OSC string in both ST and BEL terminator forms.
